title: Buttondown vs AWeber (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Buttondown vs AWeber: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between Buttondown and AWeber comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. Buttondown is built for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while AWeber targets bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Developers And Technical Writers Who Prefer Markdown

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on developers and technical writers who prefer markdown
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Buttondown wins for developers and technical writers who prefer markdown, while AWeber is stronger for bloggers and solopreneurs wanting simplicity. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or AWeber?
faq.a: Buttondown starts at a lower price point ($9/mo vs $15/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from AWeber to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, switching from AWeber to Buttondown is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
